Table of contents

    • Genetic data ID - Phenotypic data ID linking file
    • Add Health pairs data
    • Genetic ancestry
    • Polygenic score and genetic PCs

    • AID
    • Biological sex
    • Age
    • Race/ethnicity (for descriptive statistics)
    • SES variables
    • Parental relationship - parent report
    • Parental relationship - child report
    • Delinquent peer affiliation
    • School connectedness
    • ASB and SUB
    • Childhood ADHD
    • Parent report covariates
    • Child report covariates
    • Residualize PGS on first ten PCs
  • Full sample data
  • Read in factor scores generated in Mplus
  • Within family data
    • Joining data
    • Select all individuals in families
    • Select same ancestry families
    • Descriptives
  • Write data to csv for analysis
  • Population level analysis

Add Health Data Preparation for Within-Family Analysis of rGE

Author

LiChen Dong

Published

April 9, 2025

Setup

options(conflicts.policy = "depends.ok")
library(tidyverse)
library(lavaan)

path_data <- "../data_local"

Read in data

Add health in-home interview data

load(here::here(path_data, "add_health_core.RData"))

Genetic data ID - Phenotypic data ID linking file

gid <- data.table::fread(here::here(path_data, "AddHealth dbGap ID Link File.dat")) |> as_tibble() |> mutate(AID = as.character(AID))

Add Health pairs data

Here we select monozygotic twins, dizygotic twins, full siblings, and twins with undetermined zygoticity and extract IDs of individuals in those pairs.

pairs <- read.csv(here::here(path_data, "LICHENMAINNEW.csv"), header = F) |> 
         select(V2, V3, V4, V13) |> 
         filter(V3 %in% c("MZ","DZ","FS","UD", "."))

fs_dz_id <- c(pairs$V4, pairs$V13) |> unique()

Genetic ancestry

ancestry <- foreign::read.xport(here::here(path_data, "PGS_AH1.xpt")) |> select(AID, ANCESTRY) |>  mutate(AID = as.character(AID), ANCESTRY = factor(ANCESTRY, 0:3, c("eur", "afr", "eas", "his")))

Polygenic score and genetic PCs

ext_pgs_eur <- data.table::fread(here::here(path_data, "ADDHEXTPGS")) |> 
           select(IID, SCORESUM) |> 
            mutate(SCORESUM = scale(SCORESUM) |> as.vector())
names(ext_pgs_eur) <- c("GID", "EXT_PGS_EUR")

ext_pgs_oth <- read_csv(here::here(path_data, "8.14.24_EXT_PGS_PRSCS.csv")) |>
          mutate(EXT_PGS_PRSCS = scale(EXT_PGS_PRSCS) |> as.vector(),
                 AID = as.character(AID)) |>
          rename(EXT_PGS_OTH = EXT_PGS_PRSCS)
Rows: 9974 Columns: 2
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
dbl (2): AID, EXT_PGS_PRSCS

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
genetic_pc <- data.table::fread(here::here(path_data, "ADDHPCA"))
names(genetic_pc) <- c("GFAM","GID",paste0("PC",1:20))

ext_pgs <- left_join(gid, ext_pgs_eur) |> left_join(ext_pgs_oth) |>
           left_join(ancestry) |> 
           mutate(EXT_PGS = if_else(ANCESTRY == "eur", 
                                    EXT_PGS_EUR, EXT_PGS_OTH, EXT_PGS_OTH)) |> 
           select(GID, EXT_PGS)
Joining with `by = join_by(GID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
pgs_pc <- left_join(gid, ext_pgs) |> left_join(genetic_pc) |> 
          mutate(AID = as.character(AID))
Joining with `by = join_by(GID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(GID, GFAM)`

Creating variables

AID

data <- Wave1 |> select(AID)

Biological sex

sex <- Wave1 |> select(AID, BIO_SEX) |> 
       rename(sex = BIO_SEX) |> 
       mutate(sex = replace(sex, sex > 5, NA))

data <- data |> left_join(sex)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Age

age <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1GI1M, H1GI1Y, IMONTH, IYEAR) |> 
                left_join(Wave3 |> select(AID, IMONTH3, IYEAR3)) |> 
                mutate(H1GI1M = replace(H1GI1M, H1GI1M == 96, NA), 
                       H1GI1Y = replace(H1GI1Y, H1GI1Y == 96, NA),
                       IYEAR3 = case_match(IYEAR3, 2001 ~ 101, 2002 ~ 102)) |> 
                mutate(age_1 = IYEAR - H1GI1Y + (IMONTH - H1GI1M)/12, 
                       age_3 = IYEAR3 - H1GI1Y + (IMONTH3 - H1GI1M)/12) |> 
                select(-H1GI1M, -H1GI1Y, -IMONTH, -IYEAR, -IMONTH3, -IYEAR3)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
data <- data |> left_join(age)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Race/ethnicity (for descriptive statistics)

race <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1GI6A, H1GI6B, H1GI6C, H1GI6D, H1GI6E, H1GI4) |> 
        mutate(across(H1GI6A:H1GI4, ~ replace(.x, .x > 5, NA))) |> 
        rename(white = H1GI6A, african = H1GI6B, native = H1GI6C, asian = H1GI6D, 
               other = H1GI6E, hispanic = H1GI4)

data <- data |> left_join(race)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

SES variables

We use family income and adolescent reported parental education. Income will be further log-transformed in downstream analysis.

ses <- Wave1 |> select(AID, PA55, H1RM1, H1RF1) |>
       mutate(PA55 = na_if(PA55, 9996),
              H1RM1 = if_else(H1RM1 %in% c(96:99, 11, 12), NA, H1RM1),
              H1RF1 = if_else(H1RF1 %in% c(96:99, 11, 12), NA, H1RF1)) |> 
       rename(income = PA55, edu_mum = H1RM1, edu_dad = H1RF1) |> 
       mutate(edu_mum = dplyr::recode(edu_mum,'3' = 3,
                        '4' = 3,
                        '5' = 3,
                        '6' = 4,
                        '7' = 4,
                        '8' = 5,
                        '9' = 6,
                        '10' = 0),
              edu_dad = dplyr::recode(edu_dad, '3' = 3,
                        '4' = 3,
                        '5' = 3,
                        '6' = 4,
                        '7' = 4,
                        '8' = 5,
                        '9' = 6,
                        '10' = 0))
ses <- ses |> mutate(
  par_ed = NA,
  par_ed = if_else(edu_mum >= edu_dad | is.na(edu_dad), edu_mum, par_ed, missing = par_ed),
  par_ed = if_else(edu_dad > edu_mum | is.na(edu_mum), edu_dad, par_ed, missing = par_ed),
)

ses <- ses |> select(AID, income, par_ed)

data <- data |> left_join(ses)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Parental relationship - parent report

pr_parent <- Wave1 %>% select("AID",starts_with("PC34"),"PC44A") |> 
             mutate(across(PC34A:PC44A, ~replace(.x, .x > 5, NA)),
                    PC34A = 6 - PC34A,
                    PC34B = 6 - PC34B,
                    PC34D = 6 - PC34D,
                    PC44A = 6 - PC44A)

psych::fa.parallel(pr_parent |> select(-AID), fm = "wls", fa = "fa", cor = "poly")
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  1  and the number of components =  NA 
lavaan::efa(pr_parent |> select(-AID) |> mutate(across(everything(), as.ordered)), nfactors = 1:2, rotation = "oblimin") |> summary()

data <- data |> left_join(pr_parent)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Parental relationship - child report

pr_child <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1WP9, H1WP10, H1PF1, H1PF2, 
                            H1PF3, H1PF4, H1PF5, H1WP13, 
                            H1WP14, H1PF23, H1PF24, H1PF25) |> 
            mutate(across(H1WP9:H1PF25, ~ replace(.x, .x > 5, NA)),
                   across(starts_with("H1PF"), ~ 6 - .x))

psych::fa.parallel(pr_child |> select(H1WP9:H1PF25), fm = "wls", fa = "fa", cor = "poly", error.bars = T)
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  3  and the number of components =  NA 
data <- data |> left_join(pr_child)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Delinquent peer affiliation

del_peer <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1TO29, H1TO33, H1TO9, H1DS14) |> 
            mutate(across(H1TO29:H1DS14, ~ replace(.x, .x > 5, NA)),
                   across(H1TO29:H1DS14, ~ 4 - .x))

psych::fa.parallel(del_peer |> select(-AID), fm = "wls", fa = "fa", cor = "poly", error.bars = T)
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  1  and the number of components =  NA 
data <- data |> left_join(del_peer)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

School connectedness

school_con <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1ED19, H1ED20, H1ED22, H1ED23,
                              H1ED24, H1PR2) |> 
              mutate(across(H1ED19:H1PR2, ~ replace(.x, .x > 5, NA)),
                     across(starts_with("H1ED"), ~ 6 - .x))

psych::fa.parallel(school_con |> select(-AID), fm = "wls", fa = "fa", cor = "poly", error.bars = T)
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  1  and the number of components =  NA 
data <- data |> left_join(school_con)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

ASB and SUB

asb_wave1 <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1DS2, H1DS12, H1FV8, H1DS6, 
                             H1DS9, H1DS10, H1DS13, H1DS11, H1FV7, H1DS7) |> 
             mutate(across(H1DS2:H1DS7, ~ replace(.x , .x > 5, NA))#,
                    #across(H1DS2:H1DS7, ~ replace(.x , .x > 0, 1))
                    )
    
asb_wave3 <- Wave3 |> select(AID, H3DS1, H3DS5, H3DS18I, H3DS17, 
                             H3DS2, H3DS3, H3DS6, H3DS4, H3DS18H, H3HR23) |> 
             mutate(H3DS17 = case_match(H3DS17, 0 ~ 0,
                                                1:2 ~ 1,
                                                3:4 ~ 2,
                                                5:67 ~ 3)) |> 
             mutate(across(H3DS1:H3HR23, ~ replace(.x , .x > 5, NA))#,
                    #across(H3DS1:H3HR23, ~ replace(.x , .x > 0, 1))
                    )

psych::fa.parallel(asb_wave1 |> left_join(asb_wave3) |> select(-AID), fm = "wls", fa = "fa", cor = "poly", error.bars = T)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Warning in polychoric(x, correct = correct): The items do not have an equal
number of response alternatives, global set to FALSE.
Warning in matpLower(x, nvar, gminx, gmaxx, gminy, gmaxy): 31 cells were
adjusted for 0 values using the correction for continuity. Examine your data
carefully.
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  5  and the number of components =  NA 
lavaan::efa(asb_wave1 |> left_join(asb_wave3) |> select(-AID) |> mutate(across(everything(), as.ordered)), nfactors = 1:2, rotation = "oblimin") |> summary()
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

data <- data |> left_join(asb_wave1) |> left_join(asb_wave3)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
sub_wave1 <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1TO15, H1TO17, H1TO5, H1TO32, H1TO36, H1TO42) |> 
             mutate(H1TO15 = replace(H1TO15, H1TO15 == 97, 7),
                    H1TO15 = replace(H1TO15, H1TO15 >= 96, NA),
                    H1TO15 = 7 - H1TO15,
                    H1TO17 = replace(H1TO17, H1TO17 == 97, 7),
                    H1TO17 = replace(H1TO17, H1TO17 >= 96, NA),
                    H1TO17 = 7 - H1TO17,
                    H1TO5 = replace(H1TO5, H1TO5 == 97, 0),
                    H1TO5 = replace(H1TO5, H1TO5 >= 96, NA),
                    H1TO5 = cut(H1TO5, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30), 
                                labels = F),
                    H1TO32 = replace(H1TO32, H1TO32 == 997, 0),
                    H1TO32 = replace(H1TO32, H1TO32 >= 996, NA),
                    H1TO32 = cut(H1TO32, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 120),
                                labels = F),
                    H1TO36 = replace(H1TO36, H1TO36 == 997, 0),
                    H1TO36 = replace(H1TO36, H1TO36 >= 996, NA),
                    H1TO36 = cut(H1TO36, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 120),
                                labels = F),
                    H1TO42 = replace(H1TO42, H1TO42 == 997, 0),
                    H1TO42 = replace(H1TO42, H1TO42 >= 996, NA),
                    H1TO42 = cut(H1TO42, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 120),
                                labels = F))


sub_wave3 <- Wave3 |> select(AID, H3TO38, H3TO40, H3TO7, H3TO110, H3TO113, H3TO119) |> 
             mutate(H3TO38 = replace(H3TO38, H3TO38 == 97, 0),
                    H3TO38 = replace(H3TO38, H3TO38 >= 96, NA),
                    H3TO40 = replace(H3TO40, H3TO40 == 97, 0),
                    H3TO40 = replace(H3TO40, H3TO40 >= 96, NA),
                    H3TO7 = replace(H3TO7, H3TO7 == 97, 0),
                    H3TO7 = replace(H3TO7, H3TO7 >= 96, NA),
                    H3TO7 = cut(H3TO7, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30), 
                                labels = F),
                    H3TO110 = replace(H3TO110, H3TO110 == 9997, 0),
                    H3TO110 = replace(H3TO110, H3TO110 >= 9996, NA),
                    H3TO110 = cut(H3TO110, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 120),
                                labels = F),
                    H3TO113 = replace(H3TO113, H3TO113 == 997, 0),
                    H3TO113 = replace(H3TO113, H3TO113 >= 996, NA),
                    H3TO113 = cut(H3TO113, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 120),
                                labels = F),
                    H3TO119 = replace(H3TO119, H3TO119 == 997, 0),
                    H3TO119 = replace(H3TO119, H3TO119 >= 996, NA),
                    H3TO119 = cut(H3TO119, breaks = c(-1, 0, 3, 7, 14, 30, 60, 120),
                                labels = F))


psych::fa.parallel(sub_wave1 |> left_join(sub_wave3) |> select(-AID), fm = "wls", fa = "fa", cor = "cor", error.bars = T)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  5  and the number of components =  NA

Sum of sq (obliq) loadings 4.195 2.939 7.134
Proportion of total        0.588 0.412 1.000
Proportion var             0.350 0.245 0.595
Cumulative var             0.350 0.595 0.595

Factor correlations: (* = significant at 1% level)

       f1      f2 
f1  1.000         
f2  0.325*  1.000 
data <- data |> left_join(sub_wave1) |> left_join(sub_wave3)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Childhood ADHD

adhd <- Wave3 |> select(AID, H3RA1:H3RA17) |> 
  mutate(across(H3RA1:H3RA17, ~ replace(.x, .x > 5, NA)))


psych::fa.parallel(adhd |> select(-AID), fm = "wls", fa = "fa", cor = "poly", error.bars = T)
Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  5  and the number of components =  NA 
data <- data |> left_join(adhd)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Parent report covariates

Not used.

cov_parent <- Wave1 |> select(AID, PA1, PA2, PA10, 
                              PA61, PA62, PB22, # par sub
                              PC10, PC11, PC12, PC17, # know kid friend
                              PC32) |> 
              mutate(PA2 = replace(PA2, PA2 == 996, NA),
                     PA10 = replace(PA10, PA10 == 6, NA),
                     PA10 = case_match(PA10, 
                                       c(1,3:5) ~ 1,
                                       2 ~ 2),
                     PA61 = replace(PA61, PA61 == 96, NA),
                     PA62 = replace(PA62, PA62 == 96, NA),
                     PB22 = replace(PB22, PB22 >= 96, NA),
                     PC10 = replace(PC10, PC10 == 2, 1),
                     PC10 = replace(PC10, PC10 == 6, NA),
                     PC11 = replace(PC11, PC11 == 6, NA),
                     PC12 = replace(PC12, PC12 == 6, NA),
                     PC17 = replace(PC17, PC17 == 96, NA),
                     PC32 = replace(PC32, PC32 == 6, NA)) |> 
              rename(par_age = PA2, par_sex = PA1, 
                     par_marry = PA10, bad_temper = PC32)

data <- data |> left_join(cov_parent)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Child report covariates

Not used.

cov_child <- Wave1 |> select(AID, H1WP1:H1WP7, 
                              H1WP17F, H1WP17G, H1WP17H,
                              H1WP18F, H1WP18G, H1WP18H) |> 
              mutate(across(!AID, ~ replace(.x, .x > 5, NA))) 

data <- data |> left_join(cov_child)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Residualize PGS on first ten PCs

pgs_pc <- pgs_pc |> mutate(
  EXT_PGS = lm(EXT_PGS ~ PC1 + PC2 + PC3 + PC4 + PC5 + PC6 + PC7 + 
                         PC8 + PC9 + PC10,
               data = pgs_pc, na.action = "na.exclude") %>% residuals(),
  EXT_PGS = scale(EXT_PGS)|> as.vector())

Full sample data

Write out phenotypic variables only for FA in Mplus

data <- gid |> select(AID, FAMID) |> 
        mutate(across(everything(), as.character)) |> 
        left_join(data)
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
write.table(data,file = here::here(path_data, "withinfamrGE_addh_full_sample_for_fa.csv"),sep = ",", na = ".",row.names = F,col.names  = F)

names(data) |> cat()
AID FAMID sex age_1 age_3 white african native asian other hispanic income par_ed PC34A PC34B PC34C PC34D PC34E PC44A H1WP9 H1WP10 H1PF1 H1PF2 H1PF3 H1PF4 H1PF5 H1WP13 H1WP14 H1PF23 H1PF24 H1PF25 H1TO29 H1TO33 H1TO9 H1DS14 H1ED19 H1ED20 H1ED22 H1ED23 H1ED24 H1PR2 H1DS2 H1DS12 H1FV8 H1DS6 H1DS9 H1DS10 H1DS13 H1DS11 H1FV7 H1DS7 H3DS1 H3DS5 H3DS18I H3DS17 H3DS2 H3DS3 H3DS6 H3DS4 H3DS18H H3HR23 H1TO15 H1TO17 H1TO5 H1TO32 H1TO36 H1TO42 H3TO38 H3TO40 H3TO7 H3TO110 H3TO113 H3TO119 H3RA1 H3RA2 H3RA3 H3RA4 H3RA5 H3RA6 H3RA7 H3RA8 H3RA9 H3RA10 H3RA11 H3RA12 H3RA13 H3RA14 H3RA15 H3RA16 H3RA17 par_sex par_age par_marry PA61 PA62 PB22 PC10 PC11 PC12 PC17 bad_temper H1WP1 H1WP2 H1WP3 H1WP4 H1WP5 H1WP6 H1WP7 H1WP17F H1WP17G H1WP17H H1WP18F H1WP18G H1WP18H

Read in factor scores generated in Mplus

fs_par_rel_parent <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_par_rel_parent") |>
        select(V7:V8) |> 
        rename(AID = V7, par_rel_parent = V8)

fs_par_rel_child <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_par_rel_child") |>
        select(V11:V13) |> 
        rename(AID = V11, mum_rel_child = V12, dad_rel_child = V13) |> 
        rowwise() |>
        mutate(par_rel_child = mean(c(mum_rel_child, dad_rel_child), na.rm = T)) |>
        ungroup() |>
        select(-mum_rel_child, -dad_rel_child) 

fs_del_peer <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_del_peer") |>
        select(V5:V6) |> 
        rename(AID = V5, del_peer = V6)

fs_sch_con <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_sch_con") |>
        select(V7:V8) |> 
        rename(AID = V7, school_con = V8)

fs_asb <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_asb") |>
        select(V21:V23) |> 
        rename(AID = V21, asb_wave1 = V22, asb_wave3 = V23)

fs_sub <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_sub") |>
        select(V13:V15) |> 
        rename(AID = V13, sub_wave1 = V14, sub_wave3 = V15)

fs_5 <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_part5") |>
        select(V4:V5) |> 
        rename(AID = V4, par_sub = V5)

fs_6 <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_part6") |>
        select(V14:V16) |> 
        rename(AID = V14, autonomy = V15, par_involvement = V16)

fs_adhd <- data.table::fread("withinfamrGE_factor_score_adhd") |>
        select(V18:V19) |> 
        rename(AID = V18, adhd = V19)

fs_all <- fs_par_rel_parent |> left_join(fs_par_rel_child) |> left_join(fs_del_peer) |> 
                  left_join(fs_sch_con) |> left_join(fs_asb) |> left_join(fs_sub) |>
                  left_join(fs_5) |> left_join(fs_6) |> left_join(fs_adhd) |> 
          mutate(AID = as.character(AID)) |> 
          mutate(across(!AID, ~ as.vector(scale(.x))))
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`

Within family data

Joining data

data_within <- pgs_pc |> select(-starts_with("PC")) |> left_join(ancestry) |> 
               left_join(data |> select(AID, sex, age_1, age_3, white,
                                        african, native, asian, other,
                                        hispanic, income, par_ed, par_age,
                                        par_sex, par_marry, bad_temper)) |>
               left_join(fs_all) |> 
               filter(AID %in% as.character(fs_dz_id))
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
data_within |> select(where(is.numeric), -starts_with("PC"), -ends_with("ID")) |> cor(use = "pair") |> corrplot::corrplot.mixed()

Select all individuals in families

fam_id <- table(data_within$FAMID) |> names()
fam_id <- fam_id[which(table(data_within$FAMID) > 1)]

data_within <- data_within |> filter(FAMID %in% fam_id)

Select same ancestry families

#data_within <- data_within |> filter(ANCESTRY == "eur")

Mode <- function(x) {
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

# famid_na_ancestry <- data_within |> group_by(FAMID) |> 
#   summarise(fam_ancestry = Mode(ANCESTRY)) |> 
#   filter(is.na(fam_ancestry)) |> 
#   pull(FAMID) 

# data_within <- data_within |> filter(!FAMID %in% famid_na_ancestry)

# data_within |> filter(FAMID %in% famid_na_ancestry) |> print(n = 1000)
# 
# famid_ancestry <- data_within |> group_by(FAMID) |> 
#   summarise(fam_ancestry = Mode(ANCESTRY))
# 
# famid_inconsistent <- data_within |> left_join(famid_ancestry) |> 
#   filter(ANCESTRY != fam_ancestry) |> pull(FAMID)
# 
# data_within |> filter(FAMID %in% famid_inconsistent) |> arrange(FAMID) |> print(n = 100)

# data_within <- data_within |> filter(!FAMID %in% famid_inconsistent)

Descriptives

data_within |> skimr::skim()

ANCESTRY 95 0.94 FALSE 4 eur: 987, afr: 281, his: 160, eas: 66

Mean: -0.009868 (N = 1405, 184 observations removed due to missing data) 

Experimental Design: unbalanced  |  Method: ANOVA

Write data to csv for analysis

Change ancestry to numeric coding for Mplus variable definition

data_within <- data_within |> 
               mutate(ANCESTRY = factor(ANCESTRY, 
                                        c("eur", "afr", "eas", "his"),
                                        1:4)) |> 
               group_by(FAMID) |> 
               mutate(ANCESTRY = Mode(ANCESTRY)) |> 
               ungroup()

Write data

write.table(data_within,file = here::here(path_data, "withinfamrGE_addh.csv"),sep = ",", na = ".",row.names = F,col.names  = F)

names(data_within) |> cat()
AID FAMID GPAIRID GID GFAM EXT_PGS ANCESTRY sex age_1 age_3 white african native asian other hispanic income par_ed par_age par_sex par_marry bad_temper par_rel_parent par_rel_child del_peer school_con asb_wave1 asb_wave3 sub_wave1 sub_wave3 par_sub autonomy par_involvement adhd

Population level analysis

data_population <- pgs_pc |> select(-starts_with("PC")) |>
               left_join(ancestry) |> 
               left_join(data |> select(AID, sex, age_1, age_3, white,
                                        african, native, asian, other,
                                        hispanic, income, par_ed, par_age,
                                        par_sex, par_marry, bad_temper)) |>
               left_join(fs_all) |> 
               filter(!is.na(ANCESTRY)) |> 
               filter(!AID %in% data_within$AID) |> 
               mutate(ANCESTRY = factor(ANCESTRY, 
                                        c("eur", "afr", "eas", "his"),
                                        1:4)) 
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
Joining with `by = join_by(AID)`
data_population |> skimr::skim()
Data summary
Name data_population
Number of rows 7635
Number of columns 34
_______________________
Column type frequency:
character 1
factor 1
numeric 32
________________________

Variable type: character

adhd 2019 0.74 0.00 1.00 -2.17 -0.67 0.00 0.67 4.07 ▃▇▅▁▁ 
data_population |> select(EXT_PGS, par_rel_parent, par_rel_child, del_peer, 
                          school_con, adhd, asb_wave1, asb_wave3, sub_wave1,
                          sub_wave3) |>
  mutate(del_peer = -del_peer) |> 
  rename(PGS_EXT = EXT_PGS, 
         'Parent Relationship - Parent' = par_rel_parent, 
         'Parent Relationship - Child' = par_rel_child,
         'Delinquent Peer Affiliation' = del_peer,
         'School Connectedness' = school_con,
         'Childhood ADHD' = adhd,
         'Wave 1 ASB' = asb_wave1,
         'Wave 3 ASB' = asb_wave3,
         'Wave 1 SUB' = sub_wave1,
         'Wave 3 SUB' = sub_wave3) |> 
  cor(use = "pairwise") |> 
  corrplot::corrplot.mixed(tl.pos = "lt", tl.col = "black", upper = "color",
                           tl.srt = 45)

write.table(data_population,file = here::here(path_data, "populationrGE_addh.csv"),sep = ",", na = ".",row.names = F,col.names  = F)

names(data_population) |> cat()
AID FAMID GPAIRID GID GFAM EXT_PGS ANCESTRY sex age_1 age_3 white african native asian other hispanic income par_ed par_age par_sex par_marry bad_temper par_rel_parent par_rel_child del_peer school_con asb_wave1 asb_wave3 sub_wave1 sub_wave3 par_sub autonomy par_involvement adhd
